score_note: Correctly defines dominance and room/door cardinalities, but does not connect the hierarchy to the parity setup.
Identifies the minimum-image representation, but incorrectly upgrades the needed cardinality bound to equality/injectivity.
Distinguishes point and index insertion, but does not explain the door-room incidence-counting role.
Explains colorful, nearly colorful, typed missing color, and fixed-type parity bookkeeping.
Explains the colorful-to-room bridge, the cardinality sandwich, nonempty sigma, and why pick_colorful_point depends on it.
Identifies incidence counting and odd/even ingredients, but does not clearly give both final partitions and the non-colorful-room side.
Explains the unique fixed-type outside door, singleton filter, and odd cardinality-one contribution.
Correctly explains odd + even = colorful + even, yielding an odd positive colorful-room count.
Explains colorful Nonempty, use of default from Inhabited I, odd cardinality, and extraction of a colorful cell.
Covers Fcolor, grid-level Scarf, room_seq, and room_point_seq selection.
Mentions a finite image and constant subsequence, but treats it as a single color rather than the fixed color/index set C used later.
Explains outside-C coordinates tend to zero, but does not connect this to the inequalities on C that force the fixed point.
Explains compactness and shrinking diameter, but not the transfer of coordinate inequalities to the limit.
Mentions coordinate inequalities, continuity, and the limit, but does not identify the colored witnesses accurately.
Covers grid Scarf rooms, subsequences, shrinking diameter, continuity, and the final fixed point.
Explains pushing toward uniform and block correction, but does not clearly state positive block sums as the normalization precondition.
Explains projection/embedding retraction and how project_embed_id converts the lifted fixed point to a product fixed point.
Covers f_lifted, continuity, Brouwer on the big simplex, projection back, and project_embed_id.
Describes finite mixed profiles and expected payoff, but omits the no-profitable-unilateral-deviation condition.
Explains payoff improvement, normalization, continuity for Brouwer, and fixed point to Nash.
Connects product Brouwer and the nash_map fixed point, but omits the pure-to-mixed deviation step via mixed_g_linear.
Explains internal-door degree two, fiber size two, and evenness of the internal-door contribution.
Explains typed nearly colorful doors, preservation of the same missing type, colorful alternative, and room-side partition.
Connects nearly colorful rooms, exactly two doors, size-two fibers, and even incidence count.
Identifies TT as the finite grid and the indexed order, but does not explain division by l into the simplex.
Mentions coordinate bounds and diameter control, but misses outside-C vanishing and misstates the size-bound roles.
Explains stabilizing a constant color set and subsequence, but not the outside-C and inside-C coordinate estimates.
Explains outside-C zero, inequalities on C, sum-one constraints, coordinate equality, and extensionality.
Explains flat index splitting/combining, inverse properties, and their use in block sums and projection/embedding.
Explains blockWeight scaling, block sums, big-simplex normalization, and recovery by project_embed_id.
Explains normalized division, continuous numerator/denominator, positivity, and Continuous.div.
Explains the positive tPush case, but the zero branch is missing the zero-deficit/blockWeight lower-bound argument.
Explains mixed_g_linear as a convex combination of pure deviations and why pure bounds imply mixed bounds.
Mentions equivalences and fixed-point transport, but not the ProductSimplices/Fintype.equivFin interface accurately.
Explains nonnegative g_function, sum at least one, positive denominator, normalized sum one, and nash_map_cert.
Mentions profitable deviation, H1, and contradiction, but misses the fixedness plus positive-coordinate argument that forces denominator one.
Explains outside doors as empty singleton-color doors and the fixed-type singleton count that gives oddness.
Explains that two-room incidence becomes a size-two fiber in dbcountingset, making the count even.
Explains outside doors entering typed nearly colorful counting and adjacent rooms classified as same-type nearly colorful or colorful.
Misidentifies typed_colorful_room_odd as a set and does not explain odd cardinality giving a positive/nonempty filter and chosen element.
Explains stdSimplex.pick and the x_i <= f(x)_i color property, but gives the wrong reason for why the upidx witness exists.
Explains fixed C and subsequence g1, but misses their inside-C inequality and outside-C vanishing roles.
Explains y_seq as the colored witness sequence, shrinking-diameter convergence to z, and continuity transfer.
Explains outside-C zero, nonnegativity, sum-one mass splitting, and coordinate equality.
Mentions C and outside-C zero, but does not clearly connect both roles to extensional equality.
Uniform np4096 run exhausted the thinking budget and produced an empty formal response.
Explains blockSum division and nonzero denominator, but omits the Continuous.div step.
Explains embedded block sums, deficit zero, push identity, and division by blockWeight to recover y.
Correctly explains using single-simplex Brouwer on f_lifted in BigSimplex, then projecting back with project_embed_id.
Explains max 0 as profitable pure-deviation payoff improvement, but misses the later normalization role.
Explains positive denominator, nonnegative quotients, sum-one normalization, and nash_map_cert packaging.
Explains mixed_g_linear as weighted-sum pure-deviation payoffs, so pure bounds imply mixed bounds.
Explains H1 sum_g > 1, fixedness giving sigma = sigma/sum_g, self_div_lemma forcing sum_g = 1, and contradiction.
Mentions reindexing, ProductSimplices, phi, and fixed-point transport, but misstates parts of the representation mismatch.
Correctly maps dominance monotonicity to the idoor subset step and odoor index-superset step.
Correctly distinguishes the door-side cell conclusion from the room-side cardinality conclusion in an incidence pair.
Explains M_set as the extension-candidate set and m_element maximality as the dominance witness for adjacent rooms.
Links equal-minimum candidate sets and distinct neighbors, but overstates M_set disjointness as disjoint rooms.
States the two image-cardinality regimes but stops short of the injective-versus-collision door analysis.
Correctly explains propagation of the same missing type back to doors for the typed parity count.
Correctly uses the two-door property of a nearly colorful room to explain even non-colorful incidences.
Correctly explains that idoor_determines_element reduces every inserted neighbor to one of the two m_element rooms.
Explains total-order tie breaking for Scarf, but omits the TT.Ilt_keyprop coordinate-order bridge.
Identifies a dominance-to-analysis bridge, but misstates size_bound_key as a direct distance bound and omits size_bound_in/out.
Explains the finite-grid to simplex conversion and analytic use sites, but does not mention rescaling by the grid denominator.
Mentions outside-C bounds and growing denominators, but misstates size_bound_out as a distance estimate.
Explains shrinking room geometry and representative convergence, but misses the colored-witness transfer role.
Correctly distinguishes global and blockwise index representations, but does not connect that gap to single-simplex Brouwer via embed/project.
Correctly explains tPush in [0,1] as the convex-combination guarantee preserving simplex and block positivity behavior.
Correctly identifies the post-push block-mass formula as the positivity bridge before normalization.
Correctly connects positive Nat+ block weights to positive pushed block mass and well-defined projection division.
Correctly describes per-block extraction and normalization by blockSum to obtain simplex coordinates.
Correctly composes projection, f, and embedding continuity to justify applying single-simplex Brouwer to f_lifted.
States the needed project-after-embed direction, but rewrites the endpoint through an unsupported embed representation of x_big.
Correctly specializes unilateral deviation to mixedS via Function.update and expected payoff mixed_g.
Identifies the mixed payoff package and endpoint interpretation, but incorrectly says the mixed game keeps the original strategy types.
Correctly uses cg for numerator continuity, finite-sum denominator continuity, nonzero division, and nash_map_cont.
Correctly explains the maximizing pure response as an upper bound for the mixed-deviation weighted average.
Gets the nonzero coordinate witness, but reverses the payoff direction and misses the zero-improvement self-division step.
Correctly explains conjugation by phi and phi_inv and the pullback of a ProductSimplices fixed point.
